prompt:
Support keeps forwarding me screenshots of our weather-station importer dying with 'Error: parse error' and no way to tell WHICH file, WHICH line, or WHY. I've had it — we're doing error handling properly in rs-wximport, with the standard two-layer split: the library speaks a typed error enum via thiserror, the import driver adds file context via anyhow, and the typed error must survive the wrapping so tooling can downcast and react. The crate has no dependencies yet: cargo add thiserror and anyhow, pinned exactly (=2.0.18 and =1.0.103), lockfile stays committed and in sync.

The .wx format (one file per station): physical line 1 is 'STATION <code>'; every following non-blank line is a reading: '<date> temp_c=<f64> wind_kt=<f64>' (tokens separated by whitespace, key=value pairs after the date, order of the two keys doesn't matter). Line numbers in errors are physical, 1-based, blanks still count. Ranges: temp_c in -90..=60, wind_kt in 0..=250.

In src/lib.rs (split modules if you like, re-export from the crate root):
- Report { station: String, readings: Vec<Reading> }, Reading { date: String, temp_c: f64, wind_kt: f64 }, ImportSummary { files: usize, readings: usize } — all with Debug/PartialEq for test fixtures
- WxError, a thiserror enum with EXACTLY these Display strings: MissingHeader -> 'missing STATION header'; BadField { line, field } -> 'line 3: unreadable field "wind_kt=abc"' (a token that isn't key=value, an unknown key, or a value that won't parse as f64 — field carries the whole offending token); MissingField { line, field } -> 'line 2: missing field "wind_kt"'; OutOfRange { line, field, value } -> 'line 2: temp_c out of range (812)'; and an Io variant -> 'could not read <path>' that carries the underlying std::io::Error as its #[source] (callers downcast the source to inspect io::ErrorKind)
- parse_report(text: &str) -> Result<Report, WxError> and load_station_file(path: &Path) -> Result<Report, WxError>
- import_dir(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<ImportSummary> — the app layer: process every *.wx file in the directory (non-.wx entries ignored), each file's parse/read error wrapped .with_context(...) as 'importing <filename>' (just the file name, not the path). A directory with no .wx files at all is a plain anyhow error 'no .wx files in <dir>' — that one is NOT a WxError. On success count files and total readings.

tests/import.rs (plus fixtures under tests/fixtures/) is the contract: pinned Display strings, source-chain downcasts, anyhow's {:#} alternate rendering, chain().count(), root_cause() — the works. Don't touch anything under tests/. Done when cargo test passes; first build fetches the two crates from crates.io, which is fine here.
